title: Borrowing a book with zero copies left still succeeds
body: LoanService.borrowBook() decrements Book.availableCopies without first checking that a copy is actually available. When availableCopies is already 0, a new loan is still created and the counter goes negative.

title: Returning a book exactly on the due date is charged a late fee
body: FeeCalculator.calculateLateFee() treats a return on the due date itself as one day late, so members are charged even when the book was returned on time.

title: Members can register with an email already in use
body: MemberController.registerMember() never checks MemberRepository for an existing member with the same email before saving, so duplicate accounts with the same email are allowed.

title: Returned books never become available again
body: LoanService.returnBook() marks the loan as returned but never increments Book.availableCopies back up, so books that have been returned still show as unavailable to borrow.

title: Overdue reminders are sent for loans that were already returned
body: NotificationService.sendOverdueReminders() queries LoanRepository.findOverdueLoans() without filtering out loans where returned is true, so members keep getting reminder emails for books they already gave back.

title: Loan durations are miscalculated across month boundaries
body: DateUtils.daysBetween() computes the difference using each date's day-of-month field directly instead of a calendar-safe difference, so a loan from Jan 30 to Feb 2 comes out negative or wrong.

title: Borrowing an unavailable book returns HTTP 500 instead of 409
body: GlobalExceptionHandler has no handler registered for BookNotAvailableException, so it falls through to the generic handler and clients get a 500 Internal Server Error instead of a 409 Conflict.
